# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Docker Compose file for ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) and APM Server. Configure all services with network settings. Set up communication between components.
# Category: monitoring_misconfiguration

version: '3'
services:
    elasticsearch:
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:7.12.0  # Use the latest version if possible, or pin to a specific tag otherwise as per requirements
        container_name: ${PROJECT}-es-${ENVIRONMENT}   # Set unique service name for each environment in CI / CD pipelines (optional)
        environment:    # Define env variables that will be passed into the Elasticsearch Docker image. 
            - node.name=elastic1
           ...     # Continue as required by your specific use case with all necessary parameters...  
         ports:       # Expose ELK stack services to host them internally (optional) on a specified port, or bind an external IP and expose it within the Docker Network for outbound connections. 
            - '9201:9354'      ...    // Continue as required by your specific use case...  
        networks:     # Connect services in network settings (optional) if you need communication between components inside a docker-compose file..      
          default:         # Network name can be any valid Docker netowkr key. In this setup, all ELK stack related containers are connected to the 'default' or main/primary one created at startup for easy reference later on – only applicable if you have more than single network definition in docker-compose file
            elasticsearch: ${PROJECT}-es   # Use same name as NetworkKey of ElasticSearch, so they will be linked together. If not set the connection between services could fail due to absence/misalignment... (optional) – only if you are linking more than one network in docker-compose file
    logstash:     ...   # Continue with LogStash settings similar as above for setting up ES and APM server......      
      networks:         // Connect services to the elasticsearch via same NetworkKey. (See ElasticSearch service configuration)          
        default:           ${PROJECT}-net  // Reuse network key defined at first point in this setup, if not unique change it with different one as per requirement...(Optional) - only applicable when you are linking more than single networks  
    kibana:       ... # Continue KIBANA settings similar to ES and APM server......          
      networks:         // Connect services in the same network key. (See ElasticSearch service configuration)        – use exactly like elasticsearch setup          Defaults can be different than what was defined for Logstash, so please tweak as per your specific requirements...   - only applicable if you are linking more then single networks
apm:         # Configure APM server to connect with ELK Stack services (optional) – If not set the connection between components could fail due to absence/misalignment in such setup.       // Replace X-pack URL as per requirements      - only applicable if you are using x_Pack service
    apm:           # Continue APM server settings similar with ES and Logstash.....        – If not set the connection between components could fail due to absence/misalignment in such setup.       // Replace X-pack URL as per requirements      - only applicable if you are using x_Pack service
networks:         # Define custom networks (optional)   The name must be a valid Docker network key and can’t have characters other than alphanumeric or hyphens, such like ‘myNETWORK-default', 'MY2ND' etc.       // This is optional section for defining multiple isolated docker networking
    my_network:      # You may specify the name here in your Docker setup if you want to create a custom one (optional)   - Name can be any valid key that makes sense, but it must not have characters other than alphanumeric or hyphens. Example ‘my-secondNetwork', 'Second_Net' etc
    ...             # Repeat these as required by your specific use case...           // Use full paths like /path/to where needed (optional)          - If set the Docker image pulling and pushing from a custom registry will be done through this location.  Example '/mnt/registry’, 'C:/Registry' etc
